message:
Improve OAuth account selector robustness and reduce token logging

Browser automation improvements:
- Add multiple selector strategies for Google account selection:
  - div[data-email], li[data-email], [data-identifier]
  - Anthropic/Claude-specific: button[data-email], a[data-email]
- Implement fallback cascade when preferred account selector fails
- Add generic fallback selectors for any visible account

Security improvements:
- Move OAuth URL logging from Info to Debug level to avoid
  exposing tokens in standard logs
- Add sanitized Info log showing only has_preferred_account boolean

These changes improve reliability of automated account switching
across different OAuth provider UIs while reducing security exposure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-t1p8","title":"Implement depletion prediction engine","description":"# Task: Implement depletion prediction engine

## Overview
Create the prediction engine that combines current usage, burn rate, and historical patterns to forecast when rate limits will be hit.

## Technical Details

### Prediction Engine
```go
// internal/prediction/engine.go

type PredictionEngine struct {
    logScanner     logs.Scanner
    usageFetcher   *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    sessionTracker *usage.SessionTracker  // NEW: Real-time session data
}

type Prediction struct {
    Profile         string
    CurrentPercent  float64
    BurnRate        *usage.BurnRateInfo
    PredictedTime   time.Time
    TimeToDepletion time.Duration
    Confidence      float64
    Warning         WarningLevel
    DataSources     []string  // "logs", "session", "api"
}

type WarningLevel int
const (
    WarningNone WarningLevel = iota
    WarningApproaching  // < 30 min
    WarningImminent     // < 10 min
)

func (e *PredictionEngine) Predict(ctx context.Context, provider, profile string) (*Prediction, error) {
    // 1. Fetch current usage from API
    usageInfo, err := e.usageFetcher.Fetch(ctx, provider, profile)
    
    // 2. Get burn rate from multiple sources (prioritize most recent)
    var burnRate *usage.BurnRateInfo
    var sources []string
    
    // 2a. Check real-time session data first (most accurate)
    if e.sessionTracker != nil {
        if sessionRate := e.sessionTracker.BurnRate(30 * time.Minute); sessionRate != nil {
            burnRate = sessionRate
            sources = append(sources, "session")
        }
    }
    
    // 2b. Fall back to log data if no session data
    if burnRate == nil {
        if logEntries, err := e.logScanner.Scan(ctx, "", 2*time.Hour); err == nil {
            burnRate = usage.CalculateBurnRate(logEntries.Entries, 2*time.Hour)
            sources = append(sources, "logs")
        }
    }
    
    // 2c. Fall back to API-reported rate if available
    if burnRate == nil && usageInfo.BurnRate != nil {
        burnRate = usageInfo.BurnRate
        sources = append(sources, "api")
    }
    
    // 3. Calculate time to depletion
    var predictedTime time.Time
    var timeToDepletion time.Duration
    if burnRate != nil && burnRate.PercentPerHour > 0 {
        remainingPercent := 100.0 - usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.UsedPercent
        hoursUntilDepletion := remainingPercent / burnRate.PercentPerHour
        timeToDepletion = time.Duration(hoursUntilDepletion * float64(time.Hour))
        predictedTime = time.Now().Add(timeToDepletion)
        
        // Cap at window reset time
        if usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.ResetsAt.Before(predictedTime) {
            predictedTime = usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.ResetsAt
            timeToDepletion = time.Until(predictedTime)
        }
    }
    
    // 4. Determine warning level
    warning := WarningNone
    if timeToDepletion > 0 && timeToDepletion < 10*time.Minute {
        warning = WarningImminent
    } else if timeToDepletion > 0 && timeToDepletion < 30*time.Minute {
        warning = WarningApproaching
    }
    
    // 5. Calculate confidence
    confidence := calculateConfidence(burnRate, len(sources))
    
    return &Prediction{
        Profile:         profile,
        CurrentPercent:  usageInfo.PrimaryWindow.UsedPercent,
        BurnRate:        burnRate,
        PredictedTime:   predictedTime,
        TimeToDepletion: timeToDepletion,
        Confidence:      confidence,
        Warning:         warning,
        DataSources:     sources,
    }, nil
}

func calculateConfidence(burnRate *usage.BurnRateInfo, sourceCount int) float64 {
    if burnRate == nil {
        return 0
    }
    
    // Base confidence from burn rate calculation
    confidence := burnRate.Confidence
    
    // Boost for multiple data sources
    if sourceCount > 1 {
        confidence = math.Min(1.0, confidence * 1.2)
    }
    
    return confidence
}
```

## Data Source Priority
1. **Real-time session data** (most accurate, most recent)
2. **Historical log data** (good accuracy, but may be stale)
3. **API-reported rate** (if provider exposes it)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Uses session tracker when available
- [ ] Falls back to log data gracefully
- [ ] Calculates accurate time to depletion
- [ ] Confidence reflects data quality
- [ ] Warning levels trigger at correct thresholds
- [ ] Handles edge cases (no data, window reset)

## Test Cases
1. Predict with session data only
2. Predict with log data only
3. Predict with multiple data sources
4. Predict when approaching window reset
5. Low confidence when data is sparse

## Dependencies
- Create BurnRateInfo struct and calculation (caam-ef8r)
- Add depletion prediction to UsageInfo (caam-3vsd)
- Implement real-time session tracking (caam-blxo)

## Files to Create
- internal/prediction/engine.go
- internal/prediction/confidence.go
- internal/prediction/engine_test.go

 {"id":"caam-t5p","title":"Profile workspaces: Switch all tools at once","description":"## Problem
Switching contexts (home → work) requires multiple commands:
```bash
caam activate claude work-claude
caam activate codex work-codex  
caam activate gemini work-gemini
```

## Solution
Workspace presets that switch all tools atomically.

```bash
# Define workspaces
caam workspace create work \
  --claude=work-claude \
  --codex=work-codex \
  --gemini=work-gemini

caam workspace create home \
  --claude=personal \
  --codex=personal

# Switch everything at once
caam workspace work
# ✓ Switched to workspace 'work'
#   claude: work-claude
#   codex: work-codex
#   gemini: work-gemini
```

## Features
- Create named workspaces with profile mappings
- Switch all tools with one command
- Show current workspace in status
- Optional: Auto-detect workspace from directory (like direnv)

## Storage
```yaml
workspaces:
  work:
    claude: work-claude
    codex: work-codex
    gemini: work-gemini
  home:
    claude: personal
    codex: home
  
current: work
```

## Integration with Projects
Could also tie to project associations:
```bash
caam project set ~/code/work-repo --workspace=work
# Entering ~/code/work-repo auto-switches to work workspace
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:01:14.880968303Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T02:36:04.750089226Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T02:36:04.750089226Z","close_reason":"Implemented workspace command for switching all tools at once","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-t5p","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:03:33.451832994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ta7","title":"Implement provider list panel (left)","description":"## Task
Create the left panel showing the list of providers (Codex, Claude, Gemini).

## Design
- Vertical list of providers
- Currently selected provider highlighted
- Show count of profiles per provider
- Tab key cycles through providers

## Implementation
- Use bubbles/list or custom component
- Style with lipgloss (border, colors)
- Connect to main model's activeProvider

## Visual Example
```
┌─ Providers ──┐
│ ▶ Codex (3)  │
│   Claude (5) │
│   Gemini (2) │
└──────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shows all three providers
- Tab key cycles selection
- Profile counts accurate
- Visual highlight on selected","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:51:48.642239884Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T06:32:43.562092985Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T06:32:43.562092985Z","close_reason":"Implemented provider list panel with vertical layout, profile counts, selection highlighting, and integrated into main TUI layout. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ta7","depends_on_id":"caam-by4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:51:48.656836326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ta7","depends_on_id":"caam-qpx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:52:05.677440151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-tbwx","title":"Task: Local auth agent (Playwright + Chrome)","description":"Goal
- Implement a local Mac agent that completes browser OAuth flows and returns challenge codes to the remote coordinator.

Details
- Launch Chrome/Canary via Playwright with existing profile to reuse logged-in Google accounts.
- Select account using LRU strategy or explicit account list; update LRU after success.
- Extract challenge code and POST back to coordinator with request id.
- Handle retries, timeouts, and UI changes with selector fallbacks.
- Detect Playwright/Chrome availability and provide a guided install path when missing.
- Avoid logging codes or URLs except in explicit debug mode.

Tests
- Unit tests for LRU selection, retry logic, and response parsing.
- Integration tests with Playwright in headless mode using mocked pages.
- E2E test that drives a full login flow against a local fixture server.
- Structured logs with request_id, account_hint, and step timing (redacted).

Success
- Most rate-limit recoveries complete without manual copy/paste.

+{"id":"caam-tbwx","title":"Task: Local auth agent (Playwright + Chrome)","description":"Goal
- Implement a local Mac agent that completes browser OAuth flows and returns challenge codes to the remote coordinator.

Details
- Launch Chrome/Canary via Playwright with existing profile to reuse logged-in Google accounts.
- Select account using LRU strategy or explicit account list; update LRU after success.
- Extract challenge code and POST back to coordinator with request id.
- Handle retries, timeouts, and UI changes with selector fallbacks.
- Detect Playwright/Chrome availability and provide a guided install path when missing.
- Avoid logging codes or URLs except in explicit debug mode.

Tests
- Unit tests for LRU selection, retry logic, and response parsing.
- Integration tests with Playwright in headless mode using mocked pages.
- E2E test that drives a full login flow against a local fixture server.
- Structured logs with request_id, account_hint, and step timing (redacted).

Success
- Most rate-limit recoveries complete without manual copy/paste.

 {"id":"caam-tc6p","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery unit tests (state machine)","description":"Goal
- Add focused unit tests for the recover command state machine and pane transitions.

Details
- Use WezTerm mock harness to feed sequences: rate-limit -> login prompt -> URL -> code -> success -> resume injection.
- Assert correct state transitions, retries, and per-pane isolation.
- Include negative cases: missing URL, repeated prompts, noisy output.
- Emit structured test logs for each transition (pane id, state, step, reason).

Success
- State machine correctness is validated without a live WezTerm instance.","notes":"Depends on: caam-vpu2 (state machine) and caam-kbif (WezTerm mock harness). Ensure tests emit structured logs with pane id/state/step to diagnose regressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:41:26.344320711Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:01:19.343663522Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tc6p","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:41:26.347633920Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-tc6p","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:42.564726762Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-tc6p","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:48.874534533Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-tc6p","depends_on_id":"caam-vpu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:46.147978548Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
